HER2-positive breast cancer only: for patients with inadequate response to anthracycline, taxane and trastuzumab therapy

LVEF monitoring: if LVEF drops 10 ejection points from baseline and to below 50%, lapatinib should be suspended and repeat LVEF within 21 days

drugs.notes: Calvert formula using calculated GFR. AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance (seek consultant advice). Max dose 900mg (set as 890mg in ARIA for national dose bands). In 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Dose may need altering if renal function changes >10% from previous cycle.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 20 ml/min
Renal function change >10% from previous cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Recalculate dose based on updated GFR

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophil <1x10⁹/L or platelets <100x10⁹/L on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days; may resume at original dose or reduce to AUC5.5 (if starting AUC6) or AUC4.5 (if starting AUC5); stop if second episode or delay >7 days

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
special_warnings: EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before cycle 1
Carboplatin is an irritant - extravasation precautions required
Dose recalculation required if GFR changes by more than 10% from previous cycle
For obese patients or CrCl >125ml/min seek consultant advice
Consider erythropoietin per NICE TA323 if haemoglobin <8g/dL

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 loading dose: 840mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Observe patient 60 minutes after first infusion, 30 minutes after second infusion. If gap between infusions ≥6 weeks, re-administer 840mg loading dose.

Cycles 2-6 maintenance: 420mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30-60 minutes. No dose reductions permitted; if not tolerated, stop permanently. Diarrhoea can be severe - ensure appropriate anti-diarrhoeal therapy prescribed.
Cycle 1 loading dose: 8mg/kg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes. Observe 6 hours after first infusion, 2 hours after second. Dose banded per national dose bands (21mg/ml).

Cycles 2-6 maintenance: 6mg/kg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes (30 minutes if well tolerated). If missed by ≤7 days give 6mg/kg ASAP; if missed >7 days give re-loading dose 8mg/kg over 90 minutes. No dose reductions permitted.

Cycle 1: given on day 2. Cycles 2-6: given on day 1. In 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Max dose 900mg (890mg in ARIA). Calvert formula with calculated GFR. AUC5 may be used with EDTA clearance. Dose adjust if renal function changes >10%.

Cycle 1: given on day 2. Cycles 2-6: given on day 1. In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Doses >200mg diluted in 500ml NaCl 0.9%. Starting dose 55mg/m² may be considered in highly myelosuppressive patients with plan to escalate to 75mg/m². Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml). Docetaxel is a vesicant.

Cycles 7-18 (12 cycles, post-surgery maintenance): 600mg subcutaneous injection over 3-5 minutes. Alternate injection site between left and right thigh. New injections at least 2.5cm from old site. Interval between SC administrations not less than 3 weeks. No dose reductions permitted.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophil <1x10⁹/L or platelets <100x10⁹/L on day 1 - carboplatin and docetaxel
dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days. Only restart when levels reached. No haematological dose modifications for pertuzumab or trastuzumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or neutrophil <0.5x10⁹/L or platelets <50x10⁹/L for >7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce carboplatin and docetaxel to 80% of original dose
Further reduce to 50% or stop treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Docetaxel - peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 3 at 75mg/m²
Docetaxel - peripheral neuropathy grade 3 at doses lower than 75mg/m² or grade 4 neuropathy
Docetaxel - NCI-CTC grade 3 cutaneous toxicity on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade ≤1; subsequent doses reduce from 75mg/m² to 60mg/m²
Hypromellose 0.3% eye drops QDS; if ocular irritation continues reduce to 60mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment - Docetaxel: AST/ALT ≥1.5xULN and Alk Phos ≥2.5xULN
Hepatic impairment - Docetaxel: bilirubin >ULN and/or AST/ALT ≥3.5xULN and Alk Phos ≥6xULN

dose_modifications.notes: If patients do not tolerate pertuzumab or trastuzumab, treatment should be stopped

Hold pertuzumab and trastuzumab; if LVEF <40% or LVEF 40-45% and 10 points below baseline on repeat - stop. If LVEF ≥45% or >10 points above nadir on repeat - resume. Symptomatic cardiac dysfunction: discontinue, commence ACE inhibitor, refer to cardiologist.

Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting evening of chemotherapy day)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose motion then 2mg after each loose motion (cycle 1 only, max 16mg/24h)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for infusion reactions
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for infusion reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for infusion reactions
Growth factors: filgrastim/bioequivalent 30 million units SC days 3-5, 10-12, 17-19 of cycle OR lenograstim 33.6 million units SC days 3-5, 10-12, 17-19 OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2
special_warnings: HER2-positive breast cancer only - confirm HER2 status prior to therapy
LVEF must be ≥50% before starting cycle 1 of pertuzumab and trastuzumab
Cardiac function assessed prior to treatment then every 9-12 weeks and as clinically indicated
Blood pressure monitoring prior to each trastuzumab administration
No dose reductions permitted for pertuzumab or trastuzumab - if not tolerated, stop permanently
Docetaxel is highly myelosuppressive - consider starting at 55mg/m² in patients with poor bone marrow reserves
Pertuzumab-associated diarrhoea can be severe - ensure appropriate anti-diarrhoeal therapy
Symptomatic cardiac dysfunction: discontinue pertuzumab and trastuzumab, commence ACE inhibitor therapy, refer to cardiologist
Trastuzumab SC (cycles 7-18): alternate injection sites (left/right thigh), minimum 3 weeks between injections
Carboplatin max dose 900mg (890mg in ARIA); carboplatin is an irritant; docetaxel is a vesicant
Neoadjuvant protocol: 6 cycles with carboplatin/docetaxel/pertuzumab/trastuzumab IV then 12 cycles trastuzumab SC post-surgery (18 cycles total)

protocol_name: Breast Cancer - Pembrolizumab with Carboplatin/Paclitaxel and EC Neoadjuvant followed by Adjuvant Pembrolizumab

drugs.notes: Cycles 1-8 (neoadjuvant): 200mg flat dose in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 30 minutes via 0.2 micron filter. Cycles 1-4: with paclitaxel/carboplatin. Cycles 5-8: with epirubicin/cyclophosphamide. No dose reductions for pembrolizumab - withhold or permanently discontinue per immune-related toxicity guidelines.

Cycles 1-4 only. Weekly paclitaxel: days 1, 8 and 15 of each 21-day cycle. In 250ml NaCl 0.9% over 60 minutes. Must be administered via non-PVC set with in-line 0.22 micron filter. Omit paclitaxel if occurring on day 8 or 15 with low counts.

Cycles 1-4 only. AUC5 with calculated GFR. AUC4 with EDTA clearance if indicated. Max dose 750mg at CrCl 125ml/min (set as 790mg in ARIA). In 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Recalculate GFR if creatinine >1.5xULN or changes ≥10% from baseline.
Cycles 5-8 only. 90mg/m² intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded (2mg/ml). Vesicant.
Cycles 5-8 only. 600mg/m² intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded (20mg/ml).

Cycles 9-17 adjuvant monotherapy post-surgery: 200mg flat dose in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 30 minutes via 0.2 micron filter. 9 cycles (21-day cycles). No dose reductions - withhold or permanently discontinue per immune toxicity guidelines.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 1 week (or until recovery) then reduce carboplatin dose by 1 AUC; omit paclitaxel if occurring on day 8 or 15

dose_modifications.condition: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel phase: febrile neutropenia (neutrophils <0.5x10⁹/L and fever >38.5°C requiring IV antibiotics)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce paclitaxel to 60mg/m² and carboplatin by 1 AUC for all subsequent doses

dose_modifications.condition: EC phase: neutrophil <0.5 for >1 week or febrile neutropenia despite G-CSF
dose_modifications.notes: Consider 20% dose reduction for future cycles (both epirubicin and cyclophosphamide)

dose_modifications.condition: Adjuvant pembrolizumab phase (cycles 9-17): neutrophil <1.0x10⁹/L or platelets <75x10⁹/L
Hepatic: Epirubicin - bilirubin 24-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 2-4xULN
Hepatic: Epirubicin - bilirubin 52-85 µmol/L or AST/ALT >4xULN

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; may re-start when colitis at grade 1 following taper; permanently discontinue if grade 3 or above or not improving within 12 weeks
Withhold; corticosteroids 1-2mg/kg/day prednisolone equivalent; may resume if event improves to grade 0 or 1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone/day
Withhold; corticosteroids; may resume when reaction at grade ≤1 following taper

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab - NCI-CTC grade 3 or above nephritis

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; may resume when liver function at grade ≤1 following taper

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab - NCI-CTC grade 3 or above skin reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold; systemic corticosteroids 1mg/kg/day prednisolone; taper ≥4 weeks once symptoms ≤grade 1

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab - any NCI-CTC grade 4 adverse reaction

pre_medications: Cycles 1-4 (paclitaxel pre-medication, 30 minutes before paclitaxel): Chlorphenamine 10mg IV
Cycles 1-4 (paclitaxel pre-medication, 30 minutes before paclitaxel): Dexamethasone 10mg oral or IV
Cycles 1-4 (paclitaxel pre-medication): H2 antagonist per local formulary (famotidine 20mg or nizatidine/ranitidine 150mg oral or ranitidine 50mg IV)
Cycles 1-4 antiemetic (60 minutes before chemotherapy, day 1): Netupitant and palonosetron 300mg/0.5mg (Akynzeo) oral
Cycles 1-4 antiemetic (days 8 and 15): Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV
Cycles 5-8 antiemetic (day 1, 30 minutes before): Netupitant and palonosetron 300mg/0.5mg oral
Cycles 5-8 antiemetic (day 1): Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV
Cycles 5-8 antiemetic (day 1): Olanzapine 10mg oral
Cycles 5-8 take home (starting day after chemotherapy): Olanzapine 10mg oral at night for 3 days (can reduce to 5mg if sedation concern)
Growth factors cycles 1-4: filgrastim/bioequivalent 30 million units SC days 3-5, 10-12, 17-19
Growth factors cycles 5-8: filgrastim/bioequivalent SC 5 days from day 3, or pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2
Mouthwashes if required for prevention of mucositis
special_warnings: Triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) only - previously untreated locally advanced or early-stage at high risk of recurrence
Blueteq application required (PEMB21 form) - verify eligibility criteria before consenting patient
Pembrolizumab immune-related adverse reactions: can be severe or life-threatening affecting GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine, or other organ systems; early diagnosis essential
Pembrolizumab permanently discontinued for NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis, nephritis, infusion-related reactions, or any grade 4 adverse reaction
Endocrine toxicity: pembrolizumab can cause hypophysitis, hypopituitarism, adrenal insufficiency, hypothyroidism - monitor cortisol, TFTs and blood glucose
Uveitis associated with pembrolizumab - rule out metastatic disease or other ocular causes
Paclitaxel must be administered via non-PVC administration set with in-line 0.22 micron filter
Paclitaxel is a vesicant; epirubicin is a vesicant; carboplatin is an irritant
Carboplatin AUC5 max dose 750mg (set as 790mg in ARIA) - NOT in national dose banding table; verify appropriate for patient
GFR must be recalculated if creatinine >1.5xULN or changes ≥10% from baseline
Ensure patient has been given a Pembrolizumab Patient Alert Card
Three-phase protocol: Cycles 1-4 (carboplatin/paclitaxel/pembrolizumab), Cycles 5-8 (EC/pembrolizumab neoadjuvant), Cycles 9-17 (pembrolizumab adjuvant monotherapy post-surgery)
ECG/ECHO prior to starting cycle 5 if clinically indicated
FBC, U&Es, LFTs, Magnesium monitored days 1, 8 and 15 for cycles 1-2; day 1 only from cycle 3
